# make-lecture-kit — update source
#
# update.py understands a GitHub repo URL directly: it reads VERSION over "raw"
# and downloads the auto-generated zip of the default branch (main). So you just
# push normally — nothing extra to host.
#
# (Any non-GitHub value is treated as a base URL serving  <base>/VERSION  and
#  <base>/make-lecture-kit.zip , e.g. GitHub Pages or your course site.)
#
# Students then run:  python3 scripts/update.py     (or `git pull` if they cloned)
#
# Set to your repo (rename here if you use a different repo name):
https://github.com/saurabh1604/make-lecture-kit
